Open AI Context, then read the active/next rows in Pilot Gantt.

Use gate decisions to change scope, dates, or planned hours; preserve prior history.

v0.2.0 Phase Labour Summary and Gates — Historical
Planning Basis, MVP Gates, and Change-Control Rules
The Gantt workbook is the controlling source of truth for scope, schedule, progress, decisions, evidence, and next action.
The controlling baseline is an 8-week, 76-hour MVP from 2026-06-29 through 2026-08-23.
Normal capacity is 8–12 hours per week. Paid Outlier work and major life obligations retain priority.
Learning is just-in-time. Shipped functionality and validated engineering workflows take priority over repetitive exercises.
Use one branch per coherent capability, not per individual test.
Tests support the product. Add them for important behaviour, scoring correctness, failure handling, and regression safety.
Prospective work-block time tracking was waived by the user on 2026-07-15. Preserve historical values; do not request, estimate, or backfill new durations.
MVP scope is JSON artifacts, deterministic gates/checks, weighted scoring, CLI execution, result records, five cases, a small benchmark, CI, docs, and one demo.
APIs, databases, multimodal, OpenClaw, observability, deployment, governance packs, reward models, LoRA, and RL are deferred unless the MVP requires them.
Use only public, synthetic, self-authored, or authorized data. Never place confidential Outlier or employer material in the repository or workbook.
The harness supports evaluation and workflow assurance, not engineering sign-off, autonomous release, or final safety-critical decisions.
The strategic product direction is the Mechanical Engineering Workflow Assurance Platform; the current harness is its evaluation kernel. Preserve this direction without expanding the controlling MVP.

Candidate and result interfaces remain agent- and artifact-runtime agnostic, with explicit extension points for artifact references, provenance, source references, evidence locations, severity, cost/latency, and regression status.
